description: Wire together Phases 4-7 into the end-to-end deployment recording pipeline: convert forge-domain alloy types into registry-compatible core domain types, orchestrate compile → execute → parse → record, handle duplicate detection, and support dry-run mode. All pipeline code lives in a new pipeline module within treb-forge.

userStories.title: Pipeline Module Scaffold and Context Types
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the pipeline module scaffolded with context, configuration, and result types so that the pipeline orchestrator has well-defined input/output contracts.
acceptanceCriteria: treb-registry and treb-config added to treb-forge/Cargo.toml dependencies
PipelineConfig struct defined with script_path, dry_run, namespace, chain_id, script_sig, script_args, env_vars fields
PipelineContext struct defined with config, resolved script_path, git_commit, and project_root
PipelineResult struct defined with deployments, transactions, collisions, skipped, dry_run, success, console_logs fields
RecordedDeployment, RecordedTransaction, SkippedDeployment helper structs defined
resolve_git_commit() returns a short hash or empty string when not in a git repo
Unit test: PipelineConfig default construction succeeds
Unit test: resolve_git_commit() returns non-empty string in this repo
pipeline module registered and re-exported in crates/treb-forge/src/lib.rs
cargo check --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes

userStories.title: Deployment Hydration — ExtractedDeployment to Core Deployment
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to convert forge's ExtractedDeployment and ProxyRelationship into the core Deployment struct so that the registry can store deployment data with correct ID format, checksummed addresses, and all strategy/artifact/proxy fields populated.
acceptanceCriteria: hydrate_deployment produces a valid Deployment from ExtractedDeployment with correct ID format {namespace}/{chainId}/{contractName}:{label}
Address values are checksummed hex strings via Address::to_checksum(None)
B256 values are 0x-prefixed lowercase hex, empty string when all zeros
Bytes constructor args are hex-encoded, empty string when empty
DeploymentStrategy fields populated correctly for CREATE, CREATE2, and CREATE3 methods
ArtifactInfo populated from ArtifactMatch when available, empty path/compiler_version when None
ProxyInfo correctly built from ProxyRelationship for all 4 proxy types (Transparent, UUPS, Beacon, Minimal), None when no proxy
DeploymentType is Proxy when proxy relationship present, Singleton otherwise
VerificationInfo defaults to Unverified with all fields empty
Unit test: hydrate a CREATE deployment and verify all fields
Unit test: hydrate a CREATE2 deployment with salt and verify strategy fields
Unit test: hydrate a proxy deployment with Transparent proxy type
Unit test: hydrate a deployment without ArtifactMatch and verify empty artifact fields
Unit test: generate_deployment_id produces correct format
cargo check --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes

userStories.title: Transaction Hydration — Events to Core Transaction Records
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to convert TransactionSimulated events into core Transaction records and SafeTransactionQueued events into SafeTransaction stubs so that the registry can store transaction data linked to their deployments.
acceptanceCriteria: hydrate_transactions creates one Transaction per SimulatedTransaction with hex transactionId, checksummed sender, and Simulated status
Transaction deployments vec contains IDs of matching deployments linked by transaction_id
Transaction environment matches the pipeline namespace
hydrate_safe_transactions creates SafeTransaction from SafeTransactionQueued events with Queued status
SafeTransaction transaction_ids contains hex strings of all linked tx IDs
Unit test: hydrate a single TransactionSimulated with 2 deployments and verify linking
Unit test: hydrate a SafeTransactionQueued event and verify all fields
Unit test: empty events produce empty results without panic
cargo check --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes

userStories.title: Duplicate Deployment Detection and Conflict Resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to check for duplicate deployments before recording so that the pipeline can skip, error, or update when a deployment ID or address+chainId already exists in the registry.
acceptanceCriteria: check_duplicate detects existing deployment by ID (SameId conflict type)
check_duplicate detects existing deployment by address + chain_id (SameAddress conflict type)
SameId conflict takes precedence over SameAddress when both match
resolve_duplicates with Skip strategy returns skipped entries with reason string
resolve_duplicates with Error strategy returns error on first conflict
resolve_duplicates with Update strategy marks entries for update
No-conflict deployments pass through unchanged
Unit test: no duplicates — all candidates pass through
Unit test: duplicate by ID — correctly detected and skipped
Unit test: duplicate by address+chain — correctly detected
Unit test: Error strategy returns error on conflict
cargo check --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes

userStories.title: RunPipeline Orchestrator with Dry-Run Mode
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a RunPipeline orchestrator that drives the full compile → execute → decode → hydrate → deduplicate → record flow so that treb run can execute deployment scripts end-to-end with optional dry-run mode.
acceptanceCriteria: RunPipeline::execute compiles the project, executes the script, and records deployments to the registry
Failed script execution (success=false) returns an error with console log output
Events are decoded and deployments extracted, with proxy relationships detected and linked
Deployments and transactions are hydrated into core domain types with deployment-transaction linking
Duplicate detection runs before recording and skipped entries appear in PipelineResult
Dry-run mode populates PipelineResult fully but leaves registry unchanged
PipelineResult.console_logs contains decoded console.log output from script execution
PipelineResult.collisions contains any DeploymentCollision events as warnings
PipelineResult.success is true when all steps complete without error
RunPipeline and all pipeline types re-exported from treb-forge lib.rs
cargo check --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes

userStories.title: Pipeline Integration Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests exercising the full hydration → duplicate check → registry write flow with synthetic data so that the pipeline's correctness is verified without requiring real forge compilation.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 8 integration tests covering: single deployment, proxy deployment, transaction linking, duplicate skip, duplicate address, dry-run, multi-deployment, and collision reporting
Single deployment hydration + recording test verifies correct field values (ID format, checksummed address, strategy, artifact info)
Proxy deployment test verifies deployment_type is Proxy and proxy_info fields are correct
Transaction linking test verifies deployment IDs are correctly linked to transactions
Duplicate skip test verifies skipped entries appear in PipelineResult.skipped with reason
Duplicate address detection test correctly identifies SameAddress conflict
Dry-run test verifies registry is unchanged after pipeline execution
Multi-deployment test verifies all 3 deployments (CREATE, CREATE2, proxy) recorded with correct types
Collision test verifies collisions are reported in PipelineResult.collisions
All existing tests continue to pass (cargo test --workspace)
cargo clippy --workspace passes
